skill.name: specdd-debug
skill.description: Use when Agent needs to diagnose or fix a bug against active SpecDD specs.
skill.summary: Use when Agent needs to diagnose or fix a bug against active SpecDD specs.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as shell execution and text fragments as weak cryptography, but the cited lines contain documentation only. No prompt injection, malicious intent, network use, or real command execution was found. The skill has low risk because it asks agents to read SpecDD project files and make scoped code changes.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static command findings point to Markdown inline code for file paths, skill names, references, and SpecDD keywords. These lines do not instruct Ruby backtick execution or dynamic shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited backticks are Markdown formatting around paths and terms. No shell syntax, command runner, or executable script is present in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Plain Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to ordinary prose in the description and debug rule. No hashing, encryption, cryptographic API, or credential handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain plain English and skill metadata. I found no code or instruction that uses MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak algorithm.

content.value_statement: SpecDD projects can fail when code, tests, and contracts drift apart. This skill guides agents to trace failures through specs, fix root causes, and report verification.
actual_capabilities: Checks that a project has a root .specdd/bootstrap.md before use.
Resolves bootstrap files in the defined SpecDD order.
Traces the effective spec chain for target paths, tasks, or behavior.
Compares failures with Must, Must not, Scenario, Handles, Returns, and Raises entries.
Guides the smallest root-cause fix inside write authority.
Reports the contract used, root cause, changed files, and verification result.
limitations: Only applies to projects that already use SpecDD.
Does not adopt SpecDD unless the operator explicitly asks.
Depends on accessible and accurate SpecDD files.
Requires user input when the spec and desired behavior conflict.

use_cases.title: Diagnose a Contract Failure
use_cases.description: Trace a failing behavior to the governing SpecDD contract before changing implementation code.

use_cases.title: Review Test Drift
use_cases.description: Decide whether a failing test is stale, incomplete, or aligned with the active contract.

use_cases.title: Verify a Focused Bug Fix
use_cases.description: Apply a small fix and report the contract, root cause, and verification outcome.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Governing Spec
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for locating the active contract.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-debug to inspect this failure. Identify the active bootstrap chain and the governing spec before proposing any fix.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Failure Source
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for separating code, test, and spec problems.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-debug to compare the observed failure with the active SpecDD contract. Tell me whether this is implementation drift, test drift, ambiguity, or missing coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Within Write Authority
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for a scoped implementation change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-debug to fix this bug. Confirm the nearest spec that grants write authority, make the smallest root-cause change, and add focused verification.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Complex Debug Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-debug across these changed files. Resolve the effective spec chain for each target, identify any conflicts, and report the contract, root cause, edits, and verification.

output_examples.input: A request handler returns the wrong status for a documented error case.
output_examples.output: The report identifies the governing Handles and Raises entries, explains the implementation drift, lists the corrected behavior, and confirms focused verification.

output_examples.input: A regression test fails after a nearby spec was updated.
output_examples.output: The result separates test drift from product behavior, points to the active contract, and recommends updating the test rather than changing implementation logic.

output_examples.input: A desired behavior conflicts with the current SpecDD text.
output_examples.output: The agent stops before editing, describes the conflict, and asks whether the operator wants a spec change or an implementation change.

best_practices: Start with the active bootstrap chain before reading deeper specs.
Keep fixes limited to the nearest spec that grants write authority.
Report the contract, root cause, changed files, and verification result.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill in projects without a root .specdd/bootstrap.md.
Do not change specs to match broken behavior without explicit user direction.
Do not infer write authority from filenames or nearby code alone.

faq.question: What is specdd-debug for?
faq.answer: It guides an agent through debugging failures against active SpecDD specs.

faq.question: Can it create SpecDD files for a new project?
faq.answer: No. It should only run in projects that already have a root .specdd/bootstrap.md.

faq.question: Does it run shell commands?
faq.answer: No command execution is defined in the skill file. It describes a debugging workflow.

faq.question: How does it choose which specs to read?
faq.answer: It resolves the bootstrap chain first, then reads the narrow spec chain needed for the target.

faq.question: What should happen when a spec conflicts with desired behavior?
faq.answer: The agent should ask the user before editing code or specs.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
